overview:
The AI-102 exam validates the skills required to design, build, and implement AI solutions using Azure AI services. It tests a candidate's ability to integrate pre-built AI models into applications, focusing on Generative AI, Natural Language Processing, Computer Vision, and Knowledge Mining. Candidates must demonstrate proficiency in utilizing Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, and Azure AI Language/Vision services.

This certification is intended for software developers and AI engineers who have experience with one or more programming languages (such as C# or Python) and are comfortable with REST APIs and SDKs. Prerequisites include a foundational understanding of cloud computing and a basic grasp of machine learning concepts, though a deep mathematical background in ML is not required.
recertification_info: Recertify annually by completing a free online renewal assessment on the Microsoft Learn portal after your certification expires.

official_resources.title: Azure AI Engineer Associate Learning Path

preparation_tips: Focus heavily on Azure OpenAI Service and prompt engineering techniques (Few-shot, Chain-of-Thought).
Understand the difference between RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and fine-tuning for LLMs.
Practice implementing Azure AI Search, specifically indexing strategies and vector search capabilities.
Get hands-on experience with Azure AI Language for sentiment analysis, NER, and conversational AI.
Study Azure AI Vision for image analysis, OCR, and custom vision models.
Master the deployment of AI services, including regional availability and private endpoints.
Learn how to manage AI security, focusing on Content Safety and API key management.
Review the integration of AI agents and the orchestration of multi-step AI workflows.
Build a small project integrating Azure AI Search with an Azure OpenAI chat completion model.
Study the pricing tiers and SKU options for the various AI services to answer cost-optimization questions.
exam_day_tips: Carefully read the scenario-based questions; the correct answer often depends on a specific constraint mentioned in the text.
Manage your time strictly; if a coding snippet analysis is taking too long, flag it and move on.
Pay attention to the specific SDK versions or API versions mentioned in the prompts.
Use the process of elimination for multiple-choice questions, especially when comparing similar Azure services.
Ensure your identification is ready and your workspace is clear if taking the exam via online proctoring.
recommended_experience: Intermediate software development experience (Python/C#) and 6+ months of hands-on experience with Azure AI services.
